growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h computed as ln(2)/doubling_time_h. Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: Modified CMRL 1066 + 20% fetal bovine serum (Sf1Ep rabbit epithelial cell coculture, Edmondson et al. 2018)

up.method: FBA/model-derived; glucose uptake (EX_cpd00027_e0) fixed at -0.0447 mmol/gDW/h to represent minimal (non-growth) glucose consumption for maintenance under a zero-growth simulation, NOT the uptake at the 0.0231 h-1 growth condition

growth_records.cite: Shahreen et al. Enzyme-constrained metabolic model of Treponema pallidum identified glycerol-3-phosphate dehydrogenase as an alternate electron sink. mSystems (2025). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01555-24. [PMC12097511]
growth_records.snippet: biomass production flux (vbiomass) was fixed at 0.0231 h-1 ... ec-iTP251 accurately predicted experimentally observed growth rates of T. pallidum for both glucose and pyruvate uptakes ... The glucose uptake rate (EX_cpd00027_e0) was fixed a
growth_records.notes: Growth rate: paper states experimentally observed growth rate of T. pallidum was captured by ec-iTP251 for glucose and pyruvate; the biomass flux (vbiomass) was fixed at 0.0231 h-1, i.e. the experimental specific growth rate mu=0.0231 h-1 (reported). Doubling time CALCULATED: td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.0231=30.0 h. Carbon sources evaluated as sole carbon source in silico: glucose, pyruvate, mannose (mannose growth-rate prediction described as realistic vs glucose). Medium: experimental growth derives from the Edmondson et al. 2018 coculture (modified CMRL 1066 + 20% FBS with Sf1Ep rabbit epithelial cells); full component composition not enumerated in this paper, so medium_composition_stated=false. Glucose uptake -0.0447 mmol/gDW/h is explicitly the minimal NON-GROWTH maintenance consumption (zero-growth simulation to derive NGAM), not the qS at mu=0.0231 h-1; recorded with that caveat. Energy parameters (context, not yields): GAM=48.69 mmol/gCDW/h and NGAM=1.50 mmol ATP/gDW/h via Pirt equation; biomass molecular weight standardized to 1 g/mmol. Temperature, pH and O2 tension of the experimental culture are not numerically stated in this paper (oxygen inferred microaerophilic from the organism's biology). Highest proteome allocation glucose 29.6% (not a flux). Substrate-specific growth-rate bars (Fig 4D) are model predictions, not independent experimental measurements, so a single experimental growth-rate record is emitted.
